author: @TheBaconWizard
text: Still considering ideas for a tattoo made of this stuff: so far a wi-fi receiver or resonant inductive thingy (might need a piecing for that) are on the list. One "friend" suggested I could make a certain body part light-up, but after due consideration I am rejecting this idea and used some colourful language to explain this to him :)

author: @TheDuke87
text:
Have you thought about putting the ink directly into an (old) ink printer? Especially the older models should be manually refillable without the need to "hack" the device.
Of course if this works or not largely depends on the size of the particles in your ink and he respective size of the printing-heads nosle.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I have thought about it-but you mention exactly what the problems are - anyway, thanks for the thought

author: @bridgendesar
text: I wonder if you could use the ink in place of solder on a conventional circuit board

replies.author: @DanielbenDaniel
replies.text:
Robert Murray-Smith I have been gluing / soldering my wire to my antenna design. A 2.04ft circumference painted circle for 48mkz reception. I used the water prof ink. It works very nicely. I just fan the copper wires out put a drop on it & cover with a piece of tape, let it dry.  It's like a nice glue. 
Nice vid Mr Robert!.
